mechanics.rows_excerpt: TRAITS ENEMY (multiple trait) Value: 1-3 The character has attracted a enemy; either single per- son Or group. The trait is worth 1 2 or 3 points depending on the power and influence of the enemy in comparison t0 the char- acter and the organization he is part of. 1-point enemy is about as powerful as the character, while 2-point enemy more powerful than the character and 3-point enemy is much more powerful than the character. The Sample Contacts (P_ 208) can also serve as sample enemies since people likely to be valuable friends are also the kind of people that make dangerous adversaries; Enemies can provide useful inspiration for the gamemas ter'$ campaign and can serve as excellent motivators for the characters_ Enemies should get involved adventures often enough to be nuisance. but they should not dominate the group plot lines- After all, only one character took on the Enemy Trait; so that character shouldn make the whole group pay for it; If, however, the gamemaster planned on having cer- tain enemy act as nemesis for the entire group through much of the campaign_ all the characters the group could take tne same Enemy Trait to represent it. averaging 1.7 meters tall and often weighing no more than 50 kilograms_ Efforts increase perceptive ability and reaction times have had the side effect of enlarged craniums and eyes, giving pilots very distinctive appearance that sometimes caus es them t0 be shunned if they wash out of training; where they work most often in the scientist or technician castes The primary advantages pilots receive are modified Attribute thresholds in the Life Path (STR -1= BOD -1 DEX +2 RFL +21 and modified Attribute maximums as noted on the Attribute Maximums Table 56). Because they have been carefully designed and bred over dozens of generations to fly aerospace fighters pilots are considered t0 have the Natural Aptitude Trait (p. 84) for all the skills in the Clan Fighter Pilot Field In addition all pilots have the G-Tolerance and Glass Jaw traits (below) at no Character Point cost/value G-TOLERANCE Cost: character with this trait is especially resistant t0 the stress of high G maneuvering, most often found in fighter craft and acceler
